summary: Recut the SDK to the ratified one-package layout â€” the six-noun core exports from the root, the Claude Code provider face exports from the `@dtmd/temper/claude-code` subpath

per.section: Decision: one SDK package â€” the provider face is a subpath export

new.description: The `./claude-code` subpath entry module. Re-exports the first-party Claude Code provider face â€” the built-in kinds `skill`/`rule`/`memory` and their `Skill`/`Rule`/`Memory` types (moved off the root index) â€” plus the built-in floors as exported clause arrays if/when they exist in sdk/src (today only the three kinds are authored in builtins.ts; the floors join this entry when authored). This is the module the `@dtmd/temper/claude-code` specifier resolves to (50-distribution, 'Decision: one SDK package').

edit.description: Add the `exports` map: `"."` â†’ `./dist/src/index.js` (types `./dist/src/index.d.ts`), `"./claude-code"` â†’ `./dist/src/claude-code.js` (types `./dist/src/claude-code.d.ts`). Keep `main`/`types` for legacy resolvers pointing at the root. External fact: Node subpath exports, package.json `exports` field (nodejs.org/api/packages.html#subpath-exports). One package, one version â€” the engine pin (channel 2) is declared exactly once (50-distribution).

Drop the built-in-kind re-exports from the root (lines 54-56: the `memory`/`rule`/`skill` values and `Memory`/`Rule`/`Skill` types from `./builtins.js`). The root now exports only the six-noun core â€” prose, genres, needs, contracts, the generic `kind`/`genre` constructors, assembly, declarations, projection, lock, emit. The provider face moves behind the subpath (`claude-code.ts`).

Fix the stale header spelling at line 8: `@temper/claude-code` â†’ `@dtmd/temper/claude-code`, and reword to say these are the SDK's own provider-face exports surfaced through the `./claude-code` subpath (not the root). The kind values themselves are unchanged.
Retarget the provider-face imports (skill/rule/memory) from `../src/index.js` to `../src/claude-code.js`; the six-noun-core imports (harness, clause, text, etc.) stay on `../src/index.js`.
Retarget the `skill`/`rule` imports from `../src/index.js` to `../src/claude-code.js`; `emit`/`harness`/`text` stay on the root index.

tests.asserts: provider-face members author and emit unchanged when imported from the `./claude-code` subpath; core imports stay on the root

acceptance: `@dtmd/temper` root exports only the six-noun core; `@dtmd/temper/claude-code` exports skill/rule/memory; the provider face no longer resolves from the root; `pnpm --dir sdk test` (tsc + node --test) is green.
notes: Corpus Decision ratified 07-05 (PR #4, 4a3790d): one package, provider face = subpath export. builtins.ts:8 still carries the stale `@temper/claude-code`. Floors are exported clause arrays (10-contracts) but none exist in sdk/src today â€” the subpath entry re-exports the three built-in kinds; floors join it when authored. TS-only, disjoint from Rust; validated by the sdk test gate.

summary: Rewrite the gate to read declarations only from the committed lock and walk each kind's `governs` locus, dropping the copy-tree scratch import and the carriage machinery it fed

per.section: The lock and drift â€” one vocabulary

gate.reason: frame-scale demolition â€” the SDK front-door gate is discharged (07-05: @dtmd/temper published, layout ratified), so (inplace-lock-producer) no longer blocks, but dropping the scratch import re-touches ~18 test files (all set up via import::run) plus main.rs/import.rs/drift.rs/compose.rs; needs a serialized-chain decomposition ceremony before parallel-safe slices are filed (a blind single filing risks a merge-conflict loop-spin)

edit.description: Drop the copy-tree scratch import in the `Check --harness` branch (main.rs:286-292) and in `session_start_diagnostics` (main.rs:567-571); the gate reads declarations only from the committed lock (20-surface: 'the gate never reads declarations from anywhere but the lock') and walks each kind's `governs` locus for members. Retire `scratch_surface` (main.rs:1076) and the `skill_rule_corpus`/`live_extract_inplace` synthesis where superseded.

Retire the copy-tree pipeline â€” `import::run`, `write_rollup`, `write_member_surface`, `emit_manifest` â€” now that no gate path imports into a scratch surface. `init`'s in-place scan survives (re-shaped to SDK-program scaffolding, its own downstream slice).
Retire the `[[member]]` codec (`write_members`/`member_to_table`/`parse_member` and inverses) â€” the manifest dialect the retired copy-tree produced.

Retire the authored-layer carry-forward / document-carriage path (`carry_representation` and the custom-header twin) â€” dead once members come from the lock + governs walk, not a re-imported surface.

acceptance: `temper check` on a harness reads its committed SDK-emitted lock (declarations) and walks each kind's governs locus for members, with no scratch copy-tree; a harness with no lock is the unadopted case gated by the embedded default program (fail-loud), never a silent skip.
notes: SDK front-door gate discharged 07-05 (@dtmd/temper published, layout ratified); (inplace-lock-producer) no longer blocks. Remaining blocker is decomposition: the scratch drop touches ~18 test files (grep import::run/scratch/--harness) plus main.rs/import.rs/drift.rs/compose.rs â€” frame-scale. Parked for a serialized-chain design ceremony; the file list here is the truthful blast radius, not a single-tick scope.

summary: Ship the three acquisition channels â€” SDK npm package, the engine binary pinned via per-platform optionalDependencies + standalone assets, the plugin/bundle â€” fail-loud on a missing platform binary

gate.reason: needs human release setup â€” the per-platform engine-binary release workflow, marketplace publish, signing/publish creds â€” before the mechanical wrappers validate end-to-end; the SDK scope is now resolved (`@dtmd/temper` published 0.0.1 on 07-05, 0.1.0 reserved for the coordinated release that pins the engine binary), and channel 1's SDK foundation lands via SDK-RECUT-SUBPATH-LAYOUT

new.description: Build the per-target release binaries and publish them to the npm platform packages (channel 2: the engine binary pinned by the SDK via per-platform `optionalDependencies`, plus standalone GitHub release assets), and produce/publish the plugin bundle with its `marketplace.json` (channel 3). Channel auto-detected. Distinct from any CI *check* job, which is a documented two-line user-authored job (`temper check` + `emit --frozen` byte-compare), not a generated file (50-distribution, 'install is two placements').

edit.description: The root `package.json` today is the *private* flume control-plane manifest (`temper-flume-harness`, `private: true`). The npm distribution entry needs platform-specific `optionalDependencies` pointing at per-target prebuilt-binary packages plus a launcher that resolves and execs the platform binary, erroring loudly if none is installed (fail-loud, 50-distribution 'Fail-loud delivery'). Reconcile with the flume manifest (a non-private publish entry, or a split package dir) rather than clobbering it. NB distinct from sdk/package.json (the SDK authoring face, already published as `@dtmd/temper` â€” the engine pin is declared there, once).

acceptance: A stranger acquires temper via the SDK's pinned engine binary (per-platform optionalDependencies), standalone release assets, or the plugin/bundle (marketplace.json); a missing platform binary is an install error, never a silent skip (50-distribution 'Fail-loud delivery').
notes: Release infra, mostly outside src/; cargo gates can't validate npm/bundle publish; needs human creds. ch1 = SDK npm (`@dtmd/temper`, published 0.0.1 07-05); ch2 = engine binary pinned via per-platform optionalDependencies + standalone; ch3 = plugin/bundle via `temper bundle` + marketplace.json. Parked on human release creds (marketplace, signing) + the engine-binary release workflow. USPTO name screen still due before launch.
